titleStmt.title: Letter from W. Rowan to Sir Robert Chalmers, 4 July 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: W. Rowan

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
HM Prison—Halel
July 4th 1916 Sir I beg to report for the information of the
Lords Justices that the Discipline of this
prison has been well maintained by the
Governor during the past year, only in
one case was it necessary to call in the
interference of the Justices which was a
case of a violent conduct twain an Officer
of the prison—only in13 other cases
did the Governor find it necessary to
inflict punishment. 63 prisoners
have been committed to this Prison
under martial law. The garden continues to be
cultivated with the usual satisfactory results. The health of the Prisoners has been
good and the Premises kept in
excellant order. I am Sir
Your obedient Servnt
Wm Rowan Lt Col Chairman Visiting Justices</signed? To The
Undersecretary
The Castle
Dublin under Secretary
Seen and
returned
Chairman 7. 7. 16. I. Donell. 6/7 6/7 EOF 7.7. 16. I Ack
II G.B.P
EOF
5.7.16
